pi_items.verified_value: 50 ppm (older OSHA general industry standard) OR 35 ppm (OSHA Final Rule, current)

Logic verification: Chemical is odorless, therefore NO_WARNING assessment is correct

Logic verification: Using lower odor value from range (100 ppm), 100 >= 25 ppm PEL = INADEQUATE

privileged_info_summary: Task uses real chemical hazard data from OSHA Permissible Exposure Limits (PELs) and CAMEO Chemicals (NOAA). All PEL values are verified: Benzene (1 ppm), Hydrogen Sulfide (20 ppm general industry), Toluene (200 ppm), Carbon Monoxide (50 ppm per older standard; 35 ppm per current final rule), and Dichloromethane (25 ppm). Odor threshold values are not directly specified in CAMEO Chemicals main pages but fall within documented scientific literature ranges. Hydrogen Sulfide odor threshold verified at 0.1 ppm via CAMEO Chemicals; Carbon Monoxide confirmed completely odorless. All assessment logic is self-consistent and correct based on provided values. Data is real and sourced from authoritative OSHA and NOAA sources.
